flavor_text: Twisted cord and river stone for unsteady hunts.
Its pouch tears often but still finds soft throats.
Wound with thorn fiber and village superstition.
A novice stave that sings only at close range.
Swamp-drawn yew that bends for patient hands.
Light in grip and cruel in ambush.
Favored by wall scouts who never waste a stone.
Its arrows slip through brush like whispered threats.
Built for ridge hunters that strike beyond horn calls.
Chips of iron whistle before they break bone.
Dark lacquer hides the bow from moonlit sentries.
Its shafts arrive where vultures already circle.
Drawn to full arc it cracks like dry thunder.
A poacher's tool made deadly by perfect timing.
Feather-black limbs and a pull that spares no lung.
Reinforced tips punish the careless and the armored alike.
Each release feels like drawing blood from a briar crown.
A hunter's oath bound to dusk and open ribs.
Its rhythm mimics the patient heartbeat before a pounce.
Pale runes along the grip mark undefeated trails.
Its pull endures even in fire and siege smoke.
Used by wardens who leave no witness at dawn.
The shot is quiet until it ends a bloodline.
A starless relic that threads death through iron slits.
The final bow of the unseen court of cinders.
